appraisal_events.evidence: « Tu sais, j'ai raconté à Bloch... que tu ne l'aimais pas... J'aime les situations tranchées. » ... il l'avait trahie ... une expression hideuse de bassesse.
appraisal_events.explanation: Robert’s boast of having told Bloch the narrator disliked him is treated as a betrayal; the narrator explicitly condemns both the act and Robert’s self-satisfied tone and expression.

appraisal_events.evidence: Robert de Saint-Loup entrant, ce fut comme une arrivée de bonté, de gaieté, de vie... un ami qui va remuer notre vie dormante de toute son énergie, de toute son affection.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator warmly credits Robert’s presence with bringing goodness, energy, and affectionate vitality, positioning him as a life-giving friend.

status_effects.explanation: His standing in the narrator’s judgment drops due to the perceived breach of loyalty and coarse self-justification.
He gains immediate emotional leverage and centrality for the evening as the narrator feels revived and devoted to him.
